 q ΔV = ½ m v² .  (Non–relativistic)

For two particles accelerated through the same voltage (so the same q ΔV), we have  

 ½ m_e v_e² = ½ m_H v_H².

Hence  

 v_e / v_H = √(m_H / m_e).               (1)

 speed of electron : speed of H⁻ ion ≈ 43 : 1.

$$ \frac{1}{2}mv^2 = -qV $$

Since we are dealing with negatively charged particles (electron and H⁻ ion) that are being accelerated, they are moving to a region of higher potential. $$ KE = |q|V $$

⟨K⟩ = (3/2) k T ,                       (2)

where k is Boltzmann’s constant.

Set the two equal so that the ion’s kinetic energy equals the average thermal kinetic energy:

q ΔV = (3/2) k T

ΔV = 13.0 V
